race.name: L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France (formerly Granfondo Alpi del Mare)

vitals.field_size: 2,400 participants (2025 inaugural edition, actual turnout); 2026 organizer target is 2,000+
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple ristori along course and at finish

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 71.

course_description.character: Starts in central Mondovì at Piazza Ellero and finishes uphill in Mondovì's Piazza. The route loops through the Alta Langa and Monregalese valleys, including Murazzano, Vicoforte, Frabosa Soprana/Sottana and numerous smaller communes. The defining character is persistent rolling terrain — repeated panoramic climbs, descents and rhythm changes rather than one enormous Alpine pass — returning to Mondovì after approximately 110.5 km and 2,271 m of climbing. Now in its second edition (2026), the event was rebranded L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France after debuting in 2025 as Granfondo Alpi del Mare — the same race, course region and organizer, under official Tour de France/L'Étape branding for its second year.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Alta Langa/Monregalese 'sawtooth' — continuous climbing and descending throughout the route, making pacing and repeated accelerations the decisive factor rather than any single summit.
suffering_zones: Oberti — 1.8 km gaining 160 m, approximately 8.9% average; the designated mountains-jersey climb
Sbaranzo — new climb added for the 2026 route; length and gradient not yet published
The Alta Langa/Monregalese sawtooth — see signature_challenge
The late-season return through the Monregalese valleys — accumulated climbing, not altitude, is the principal difficulty here

logistics.lodging_strategy: Mondovì and surrounding Alta Langa region offer accommodation options. The event weekend (September 19-21, 2026) includes festival activities, suggesting local hospitality infrastructure. Specific recommendations not detailed in official materials.

history.origin_story: Debuted in 2025 as Granfondo Alpi del Mare, an independent Mondovì granfondo; rebranded L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France for its 2026 second edition, joining the official L'Étape Italy calendar alongside L'Étape Piemonte (Entracque).

biased_opinion.verdict: A Promising Debut, Now Under Official Tour de France Branding
biased_opinion.summary: This race debuted in 2025 as Granfondo Alpi del Mare and drew 2,400 riders in its first year through the Alta Langa and Monregalese valleys — the organizer's 2026 target of 'over 2,000' is actually a step down from that actual 2025 turnout. For 2026, it was absorbed into the official L'Étape Italy calendar and rebranded L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France — the same course region and organizer, now carrying Tour de France/L'Étape closed-road protection and chip timing. As a second-year event under new branding, it lacks a long operating history, and its own official pages already disagree with each other on the basics: the event homepage shows 2,201 m of climbing while the route page, FAQ and regulations all say 2,271 m.
strengths: Official Tour de France/L'Étape affiliation as of 2026, with closed-road protection for riders inside the race convoy
Scenic Alta Langa and Monregalese roads with a gourmet Murazzano feed zone
Persistent rolling terrain that rewards pacing and repeated accelerations, not just raw climbing power
A shorter, e-bike-compatible Cicloturistica option (60.35 km) alongside the full Granfondo
weaknesses: Only the second edition overall (first as L'Étape Mondovì), so it lacks a long operating history
The organizer's own pages already conflict on basics — 2,201 m vs. 2,271 m of climbing across different official pages
Roads are closed only within the moving race envelope; riders passed by the end vehicle must continue under normal traffic rules
Competitive entry requires cycling-specific medical certification
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A well-located Piedmont climbing test that just gained real Tour de France pedigree — worth watching as it establishes itself, but confirm the current year's exact vitals directly given the organizer's own pages don't agree.

final_verdict.one_liner: A well-attended gran fondo through the Alta Langa and Monregalese hills — now officially L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France as of its 2026 second edition.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want genuine Alta Langa/Monregalese climbing with new Tour de France/L'Étape pedigree. Confirm current-year climbing figures directly — the organizer's own pages disagree by 70 m.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the other 2026 Piedmont L'Étape stop, see L'Étape Piemonte (Entracque, May) — also in this database.

videos.title: Granfondo Alpi del Mare 2025: il trailer ufficiale del debutto a Mondovì
videos.description:
Granfondo Alpi del Mare 2025: il trailer ufficiale della prima edizione a Mondovì

Dal 19 al 21 settembre 2025 Mondovì ospiterà la Granfondo Alpi del Mare, evento ciclistico su strada articolato in due percorsi – competitivo e cicloturistico – con partenza e arrivo nella città piemontese.

Nel video ufficiale: anticipazioni sul tracciato, dettagli della maglia celebrativa, panoramiche dell’ambiente attraversato e riferimenti al programma dell’intero fine settimana.

Percorsi:

Granfondo (cronometrata): 101,4 km, 2.002 m d+ attraverso l’Alta Langa, la Valle Corsaglia e la salita al Santuario di Vicoforte.

Cicloturistica (non competitiva): 58,8 km, 1.114 m d+, rivolta a ciclisti amatori e appassionati.

La maglia ufficiale dell’evento sarà consegnata a tutti gli iscritti alla Granfondo. Il disegno richiama le tonalità del paesaggio tra Alpi Liguri e colline monregalesi.

Il programma prevede anche: expo village, talk tematici, street food, gimcana per bambini, Music Run, momenti musicali e cerimonia finale.

📅 Data: 19-21 settembre 2025
📍 Luogo: Mondovì (CN)
📌 Organizzazione: GF Alpi del Mare con il patrocinio delle istituzioni locali e il supporto di partner tecnici

🔗 Informazioni e iscrizioni su: www.granfondoalpidelmare.com

#GranfondoAlpidelMare #Mondovì #Granfondo #Ciclismo #Cicloturismo #Alpi #Piemonte #EventiCiclismo #AltaLanga #Vicoforte #BikeFestival #CiclismoItalia #MagliaGranfondo #WeekendBike #CiclismoAmatoriale #AlpidelMare2025

videos.title: Granfondo Alpi del Mare 2025
videos.description:
La Granfondo Alpi del Mare 2025 ha riportato il grande ciclismo a Mondovì, trasformando la città e il suo territorio in un palcoscenico di sport, passione e partecipazione.

Partenza e arrivo nel cuore di Mondovì, un percorso capace di esaltare le bellezze delle Langhe e dei comuni attraversati. L’edizione 2025 ha consolidato l’evento come appuntamento di riferimento nel calendario granfondistico.
Un successo che rafforza una visione precisa: far crescere anno dopo anno un’esperienza capace di unire atleti, territorio e comunità. Prossimo appuntamento 20 settembre 2026.

rider_intel.search_text: The Granfondo Alpi del Mare in Mondovi, Piedmont presents riders with significant heat conditions according to participant accounts. Riders repeatedly emphasize the challenging heat encountered during the 68.8-mile course through the Italian Alps. The mountainous terrain of Piedmont appears to amplify temperature concerns, with multiple mentions of heat being a primary factor riders face. While specific course details are limited in available footage, the consistent emphasis on thermal conditions suggests heat management becomes a critical consideration for participants tackling this 7,451-foot elevation gain event. The alpine setting of this Tier 2 gravel race appears to create demanding weather conditions that riders must prepare for when racing through the Italian countryside.

eligibility.notes: Confirmed rebranded to L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France for its second edition, Sunday 20 September 2026, registrations open. Vitals confirmed: Granfondo 110.5 km/2,271 m, Cicloturistica 60.35 km/1,104 m. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research as part of the Roadie Labs editorial wave 3.

catalog_flags.status_note: Not a duplicate of race-data/letape-piemonte.json — this is a separate 2026 Piedmont stop (Mondovì, September) rebranded L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France, distinct from L'Étape Piemonte (Entracque, May). Both trace to the same 2026 A.S.O./L'Étape Italy relocation that discontinued L'Étape Parma (see race-data/letape-parma.json).
